title: panicum crus-galli var. edule
intro: Panicum crus-galli var. edule, also known as Japanese millet or Chinese barnyard grass, is a type of wild grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, and is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, providing health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. The herb can be prepared in various ways, including tea, infusion, and decoction, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine.
